title: Filippo Brunelleschi: The Buildings By Howard Saalman
highlights:
Filippo Brunelleschi's few but seminal buildings have stood as touchstones of a "return to Antiquity" in  ...    Filippo Brunelleschi's few but seminal buildings have stood as touchstones of a "return to Antiquity" in  ...    Howard Saalman offers here a definitive modern study of Brunelleschi's buildings, based on detailed archaeological investigation of the monuments and new exhaustive research in the Florentine archives. Saalman reassesses Brunelleschi's architectural work in the context of the political, economic, and religious environment of early fifteenth-century Florence. He reexamines Brunelleschi's personal style of designing details and of managing the  ...    Howard Saalman is Andrew Mellon Professor of Architecture at Carnegie Mellon University and author of several books, including*Filippo Brunelleschi: The Cupola of Santa Maria del Fiore*(1980) and an annotated edition of*The Life of Brunelleschi by Antonio Manetti*(Penn State, 1970).
